title: Avoid loading registry config when not needed
body: Loading the registry config (in particular the shortname config on rhel) is quite slow, and it happens even if you run `podman run --pull=never`. Ideally it should be faster, but even better would be to not load this config at all when `pullPolicy == config.PullPolicyNever`.

comments.body: Looking into this it seems we may need the shortname aliases to be able to run an image based on a short name maybe? Can we detect when we have a match without shortnames first and only load the config otherwise?

A reproducer would be ideal but I assume you are using a short name:
```
# WITH SHORT NAMES
~ $ strace -fff podman run --rm alpine true 2>&1 | grep shortnames.conf
[pid 173881] openat(AT_FDCWD, "/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/000-shortnames.conf", O_RDONLY|O_CLOEXEC) = 8
# WITHOUT SHORT NAME
$ strace -fff podman run --rm docker.io/library/alpine:latest true 2>&1 | grep shortnames.conf
```

The conf is only loaded when Podman actually needs to resolve a short name.  Using FQNs or image IDs won't trigger the code path.

So tests should move to 

```
# echo $(date "+-%s.%N +"; ./bin/podman run --pull=never --network=host --security-opt seccomp=unconfined registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:latest date "+%s.%N") | bc -q
.115217529
```
Yes, that will avoid the expensive lookup.
